Fitaviz 1 Overview We derive steps from published methods to process, visualize and extract activity summary statistics from Fitbit data. The focus is on adequately capturing activity patterns that ultimately support epidemiological findings. 1.1 Methods Extensive use of accelerometers in health research has led to some level of agreement of which issues processing of epoch accelerometer values should address, most notably how to determine non-wear time and what constitutes a valid or adherent day. We collect processing decisions from the literature, mostly approaches widely adopted for ActiGraph’s activity counts, and illustrate their application on Fitbit’s steps and HR minute data. Wear-time non-wear detection with activity counts is typically based on the length of periods of low acceleration. As for FitBit, minutes with non-empty HR and non-empty steps are labeled as wear-time. Wear minutes between 8am and 8pm are summed across each day to obtain daytime wear. Adherent day to account for potential wear-time diurnal bias, adherent days are days with at least 10 hours of daytime wear. Adherent time refers to wear minutes within an adherent day. Fig. 1 Depiction of daytime wear. For each day, wear minutes are aggregated according to their physical activity classification. Fitbit’s proprietary algorithm classifies each minute in four activity-levels: sedentary, light, moderate and vigorous. The first panel shows daily total counts in adherent and non-adherent days, the second panel shows the derived proportion of time spent in each activity-level. On adherent days the proportions of time spent in each activity-level are representative of diurnal activity, and are likely misrepresentations of activity on non-adherent days.

2 Summaries Data Dictionary patients_summary.csv patient level entries summary stats computed for adherent wear time AWT and non-adherent wear NAW daily_data.csv patient-day level entries only adherent days included daily_summary.csv patient level entries summary stats computed only for adherent wear time AWT 2.1 patients_summary.csv Summary describing patient’s wear and activity from min_date to max_date. Following the steps described in Methods, daytime wear minutes are labeled and classified as adherent time or non-adherent time. Summary stats are computed across all the patient’s adherent wear time AWT or non-adherent wear NAW, column names make the distinction. Column name Description 1 id Patient id. 2 min_date Patient’s first day in the study (or data export). 3 max_date Patient’s last day in the study (or data export). 4 daytime_hours Total number of daytime hours (between 8am and 8pm) in the study. 5 wear_daytime_hours Total number of daytime wear minutes (minutes with non-missing HR and non-missing step) aggregated in hours. 6 adherent_wear_hours Total number of daytime minutes that are adherent aggregated in hours. 7 nonadh_wear_hours Total number of daytime minutes that are non-adherent aggregated in hours. 8 daytime_wear_pct Proportion of weartime in the study during daytime. 9 nonadherent_ratio Ratio of total non-adherent over adherent time. 10 min_HR__<AWT, NAW> Patient’s minimum HR per minute value (average) within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 11 max_HR__<AWT, NAW> Patient’s maximum HR per minute value (average) within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW) minutes. 12 mean_HR__<AWT, NAW> Patient’s mean HR per minute value (average) within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW) minutes. 13 max_pos_steps__<AWT, NAW> Patient’s maximum number of steps per minute (count) within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 14 mean_pos_steps__<AWT, NAW> Patient’s average number of steps per minute (count) within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 15 zero_steps_prop__<AWT, NAW> Proportion of minutes with zero steps within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 16 sedentary_prop__<AWT, NAW> Proportion of sedentary minutes within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 17 light_prop__<AWT, NAW> Proportion of light activity minutes within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 18 moderate_prop__<AWT, NAW> Proportion of moderate activity minutes within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 19 active_prop__<AWT, NAW> Proportion of active activity minutes within the adherent weartime (AWT) or non-adherent weartime (NAW). 2.2 daily_data.csv This summary contains per-day values across adherent days. Column name Description 1 id Patient’s id. 2 date Date. 3 index Number of days since first day in study (or since first date in the data export). 4 adherent_hours Total number of adherent minutes in `date` aggregated to hours. 5 mean_HR Average HR of adherent weartime minutes in `date`. 6 sd_HR HR’s standard dev of adherent weartime minutes in `date`. 7 zero_step_prop Proportion of adherent weartime minutes in `date` with zero steps. 8 sedentary_prop Proportion of adherent weartime minutes in `date` with sedentary activity. 9 light_prop Proportion of adherent weartime minutes in `date` with light activity. 10 moderate_prop Proportion of adherent weartime minutes in `date` with moderate activity. 11 active_prop Proportion of adherent weartime minutes in `date` with active activity. 2.3 daily_summary.csv Patients’ summary statistics of daily physical activity in adherent days. Column name Description 1 id Patient’s id. 2 mu_sedentary_prop Daily average (sample mean estimate) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in sedentary activity. 3 mu_light_prop Daily average (sample mean estimate) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in light activity. 4 mu_moderate_prop Daily average (sample mean estimate) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in moderate activity. 5 mu_active_prop Daily average (sample mean estimate) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in active activity. 6 sd_error_sedentary_prop Standard error (of the sample mean) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in sedentary activity. 7 sd_error_light_prop Standard error (of the sample mean) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in light activity. 8 sd_error_moderate_prop Standard error (of the sample mean) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in moderate activity. 9 sd_error_active_prop Standard error (of the sample mean) of the proportion of adherent weartime spent in active activity.
